description:
The Office of Congressman Brian Mast (R-FL-21) is currently accepting applications for unpaid internships at the Washington, D.C. member office during the spring 2025 term. This internship will run from early February through May.

The ideal candidate will be able to work in a fast-paced environment, have a general understanding of the legislative process, and possess strong written and oral communication skills. Florida ties are not necessary but preferred. The internship schedule is flexible for college students if needed.
responsibilities: Answer and log constituent phone calls
Assist staff with administrative duties and projects
qualifications: Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
General understanding of the legislative process
Strong written and oral communication skills
Florida ties preferred but not required
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email their resumes and a brief writing sample to joseph.harrington@mail.house.gov. Please use the subject line 'LASTNAME - Spring 2025 internship'.

office: The Democratic Staff of the U.S. House Select Committee on Strategic Competition between the United States and the Chinese Communist Party
description:
The Democratic Staff of the U.S. House Select Committee on Strategic Competition between the United States and the Chinese Communist Party seeks qualified undergraduate students and graduates for paid internships beginning as early as January 20th.

This position is hybrid (in-office and remote).

Responsibilities include, but are not limited to, assisting Committee staff in preparing for congressional hearings, conducting legislative research, providing support to investigations, drafting memoranda, and performing other policy and administrative tasks as necessary.

Interns play a key role in the office and will work closely with Committee counsel and staff. Our ideal candidates are organized, committed, and eager to join a fast-paced environment and build upon their knowledge of how Congress works—in particular, the role that congressional committees play in the federal legislative process.

Ideal candidates possess strong organizational, communication, research and writing skills, as well as the ability to manage multiple tasks and work well under pressure in a team environment.

Candidates with diverse backgrounds and experiences are strongly encouraged to apply. A background and/or interest in subject areas related to the People's Republic of China is strongly preferred.
responsibilities: Assist Committee staff in preparing for congressional hearings
Perform other policy and administrative tasks as necessary
qualifications: Organized, committed, and eager to join a fast-paced environment
Strong organizational, communication, research, and writing skills
Ability to manage multiple tasks and work well under pressure in a team environment
Background and/or interest in subject areas related to the People's Republic of China strongly preferred
how_to_apply: Submit a resume, cover letter, and one writing sample (two pages max.) to SCCDems.resumes@mail.house.gov. Please put "Spring 2025 Intern Application" in the subject line. In your cover letter, please indicate preferred start/end dates for scheduling purposes.
